title: TangentRelationships Object
description: The collection of Tangent Relationships in this component. This provides access to all existing tangent relationships and supports the ability to create new tangent relationships.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new tangent relationship between two components.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a TangentRelationshipInput object, which is the API equivalent to the Tangent Relationship command dialog. You use methods and properties on the returned class to set the desired options, similar to providing input in the Tangent Relationship command dialog. Once the settings are defined you call the TangentRelationships.add method passing in the TangentRelationshipInput object to create the actual TangentRelationship.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified tangent relationship using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified tangent relationship using a name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns number of TangentRelationship objects in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: TangentRelationships.add Method
add.description: Creates a new tangent relationship between two components.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created TangentRelationship or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The TangentRelationshipInput object that defines the geometry and various inputs that fully define a tangent relationship. A TangentRelationshipInput object is created using the TangentRelationships.createInput method.

classType.title: TangentRelationships.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: TangentRelationships.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a TangentRelationshipInput object, which is the API equivalent to the Tangent Relationship command dialog. You use methods and properties on the returned class to set the desired options, similar to providing input in the Tangent Relationship command dialog. Once the settings are defined you call the TangentRelationships.add method passing in the TangentRelationshipInput object to create the actual TangentRelationship.
createInput.return_description: Returns the TangentRelationshipInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: faceOne
parameters.description: A BRepFace object that will remain tangent to the set of specified tangent faces.

parameters.name: tangentFaces
parameters.description: A single BRepFace object that is part of the body that faceOne will remain tangent to. All of the faces of the body will be used when computing the tangent relationship.

item.title: TangentRelationships.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified tangent relationship using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: TangentRelationships.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified tangent relationship using a name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the item within the collection to return.

count.title: TangentRelationships.count Property
count.description: Returns number of TangentRelationship objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = tangentRelationships_var.count

isValid.title: TangentRelationships.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = tangentRelationships_var.isValid

objectType.title: TangentRelationships.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = tangentRelationships_var.objectType
